summary: DressVibe backend (FastAPI + Mongo + Emergent LLM/Gemini Nano Banana) tested end-to-end via external preview URL. All 24 pytest cases pass (100%) — auth, garments CRUD, generations (real AI, 2 variations), studio edit (real AI), caption (AI with fallback), virtual clients CRUD, stats, categories, and authorization enforcement on all protected endpoints.

minor.issue: When no Authorization header is sent AND the JSON body is missing required fields, FastAPI returns 422 (Pydantic validation) before the Header dependency fires — so a totally anonymous, malformed request leaks the request schema instead of a flat 401. To return 401 first, the auth dependency should be declared via Depends() at the endpoint signature (before the body) or via a middleware. Functionally non-blocking: anonymous requests with a valid-shape body do correctly get 401.
Success responses return HTTP 200 instead of the more conventional 201 (create) / 204 (delete). Cosmetic — clients work fine.

If the upstream Gemini call fails partially (e.g. budget/rate errors observed in backend logs: 'litellm.BadRequestError: Budget has been exceeded'), generations endpoint returns 200 with status='failed' and an empty images list — frontend should handle this. Studio returns 502 'Modifica non riuscita, riprova' which is handled correctly.

action_items: (Optional polish) Move auth dependency into FastAPI Depends() at the function signature so 401 fires before Pydantic 422 on anonymous malformed POSTs.
(Optional polish) Return 201 on create and 204 on delete to follow REST conventions.

Monitor EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget — logs show a transient 'Budget has been exceeded' message during the run, although the test-time generation/studio calls still succeeded. If hit again, generations will return status='failed' and studio/edit 502; the UI must surface a friendly Italian error in both cases.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 552 lines — borderline. Acceptable for first cut, but route groups (auth, garments, generations, studio, clients) could be split into routers as the app grows.
All Mongo finds correctly project out `_id`. Verified no ObjectId leak in any tested response.
`get_current_user` correctly handles missing/invalid/expired sessions; expires_at timezone-naive case is also handled.
`generate_single_image` swallows all exceptions and returns None — good for the parallel asyncio.gather pattern, but the final status='failed' branch should ideally include a reason/diagnostic.
`build_outfit_prompt` uses Variation seed in the text but image models don't deterministically seed from prose; OK as a small diversity nudge.
Caption endpoint has a graceful Italian fallback if AI fails — nice touch.
CORS is wide-open (allow_origins=['*']) which is fine for development but should be tightened for production.
seed_data_creation: Per-test-session: one TEST_ user and one TEST_ session_token inserted directly into MongoDB (db=dressvibe, collections users + user_sessions) via the conftest fixture. All TEST_ garments/generations/clients are deleted automatically in the session teardown. No production-like data is touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use the existing /app/backend/tests/ pytest suite — it auto-seeds a TEST_ user + bearer token in Mongo via conftest and cleans up. Base URL is read from the EMERGENT preview host (hardcoded to https://outfit-gen-11.preview.emergentagent.com per /app/frontend/.env EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL). Run with: `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/ -v`. Generation + Studio tests take ~20-40s each because they hit the real Gemini Nano Banana model. If EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget is exhausted, those two tests will skip (502) or assert on status='failed'. Frontend (Expo) has not been tested in this iteration.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. All 5 initial failing tests were test-side issues (anonymous POST with empty body triggered Pydantic 422 before the auth dependency). Fix applied: parametrized tests now send a schema-valid body so the auth path is actually exercised; all 24 tests subsequently pass.
